mapping.a_element: large matrix to be compressed (the environment/corner tensors of the iPEPS contraction)
mapping.b_element: the user-item rating/preference matrix being approximated

mapping.a_element: retained low-rank subspace of bond dimension chi
mapping.b_element: the fitted low-rank projection subspace of the sketch

mapping.a_element: goal of cutting the cost of a repeated large-matrix operation
mapping.b_element: goal of cheap on-the-fly embedding recompute without retraining

brief_justification: The only genuine overlap is the generic goal of low-rank approximation of a large matrix; the actual transferable machinery differs. Paper A's mechanism is deterministic subspace iteration — a QR-based projector that iteratively refines the dominant singular subspace to replace a large SVD — whereas Paper B's mechanism is a one-shot low-rank projection combined with norm-proportional importance sampling over a KP-tree/segment-tree data structure yielding a monotone incremental error envelope. Iterative QR subspace refinement and importance-sampling streaming sketches are different techniques, so no role-for-role mapping of the driving process holds.
